name: TREND
full_name: Transparent Reporting of Evaluations with Nonrandomized Designs
description: Checklist for quasi-experimental and non-randomized intervention studies (TREND 2004)

items.description: Information on how unit were allocated to interventions
items.notes: Title should identify the non-randomized design

items.description: Structured abstract with design, methods, results, conclusions
items.notes: Structured abstract recommended

items.description: Scientific background and rationale for study
items.notes: Theoretical or conceptual framework for the intervention

items.description: Theories used in designing behavioral interventions
items.notes: Theoretical basis for behavioral and public health interventions

items.description: Eligibility criteria for participants, including criteria at different levels in recruitment/sampling plan
items.notes: Multi-level eligibility criteria for cluster/community interventions

items.description: Settings and locations where the data were collected
items.notes: Settings are particularly important for community-based interventions

items.description: Details of the interventions intended for each study condition and how and when they were actually administered, specifically including: content, delivery method, unit of delivery, deliverer, setting, exposure quantity and duration, time span, activities to increase compliance or adherence
items.notes: CRITICAL: Detailed intervention description is essential for non-randomized designs

items.description: Specific objectives and hypotheses
items.notes: Clear a priori hypotheses

items.description: Clearly defined primary and secondary outcome measures
items.notes: Pre-defined primary and secondary outcomes

items.description: How sample size was determined and, when applicable, explanation of any interim analyses and stopping rules
items.notes: Sample size justification including design effect for clustered designs

items.description: Unit of assignment (the unit being assigned to study condition, e.g., individual, group, community)
items.notes: Unit of assignment is critical for non-randomized designs

items.description: Method by which units were assigned to study conditions, including details of any restriction
items.notes: Non-random assignment method must be clearly described

items.description: Inclusion of aspects employed to help minimize potential bias induced due to non-randomization
items.notes: CRITICAL: Methods to address confounding in non-randomized designs

items.description: Whether or not participants, those administering the interventions, and those assessing the outcomes were blinded to study condition assignment
items.notes: Blinding status particularly important when not randomized

items.description: Statistical methods used to compare study groups for primary outcome(s)
items.notes: Statistical methods must account for non-randomized design

items.description: Statistical methods used for additional analyses (subgroup analyses and adjusted analyses)
items.notes: Sensitivity and subgroup analyses to test robustness

items.description: Flow of participants through each stage of the study — enrollment, assignment, allocation, and analysis
items.notes: TREND flow diagram with participant numbers at each stage

items.description: Baseline demographic and clinical characteristics of each group
items.notes: Baseline comparisons crucial for non-randomized studies

items.description: Data on study group equivalence at baseline and statistical methods used to control for baseline differences
items.notes: Assessment of baseline equivalence is fundamental in non-randomized designs

items.description: For each primary and secondary outcome, a summary of results for each study condition, and the estimated effect size and a confidence interval
items.notes: Effect sizes with confidence intervals for all outcomes

items.description: Summary of other analyses performed, including subgroup and sensitivity analyses
items.notes: Additional analyses to assess robustness of findings

items.description: Summary of all important adverse events or unintended effects in each study condition
items.notes: Adverse events must be reported for interventional studies

items.description: Interpretation of the results, taking into account study hypotheses, sources of potential bias, imprecision of measures, and the dangers associated with multiplicity of analyses and outcomes
items.notes: Must address confounding and bias due to non-randomization

items.description: Discussion of results in relation to non-randomized design, and discussion of potential sources of bias or imprecision. Include barriers to intervention implementation
items.notes: CRITICAL: Must explicitly discuss limitations of non-randomized design

items.description: Generalizability (external validity) of the trial findings
items.notes: External validity considerations for non-randomized evidence
